summary: Matched the 320-byte common fighter HammerLanding code by moving five functions out of asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s into a new src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_HammerLanding.c translation unit, adding a dedicated header, wiring the object into obj_files.mk, and refining related common-fighter motion/common-data types. The matched functions were ftCo_800C5F88, ftCo_HammerLanding_Anim, ftCo_HammerLanding_IASA, ftCo_HammerLanding_Phys, and ftCo_HammerLanding_Coll, all reported as 100.00% matched. No review comments were present in the slice.
what_changed: Deleted the HammerLanding assembly block from asm/melee/ft/ft_0C31.s, including ftCo_800C5F88, ftCo_HammerLanding_Anim, ftCo_HammerLanding_IASA, ftCo_HammerLanding_Phys, ftCo_HammerLanding_Coll, and their local sdata2 constants.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_HammerLanding.c implementing the matched HammerLanding functions.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_HammerLanding.h with address-annotated prototypes and include guard GALE01_0C5F88.
Moved the HammerLanding prototypes out of the broader src/melee/ft/ft_0C31.h header into the new specific header.
Added ftCo_HammerLanding.c.o to TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk before the remaining ft_0C31.s object.
Included ftCo_HammerLanding.h from ftCo_HammerFall.c and ftCo_Init.c so callers/init tables can use the new declarations.
Extended union ftCommon_MotionVars with a hammerlanding struct containing x0 at fp+2340 and float x4 at fp+2344.
Refined ftCommonData field x6B4 from UNK_T to int, matching its use as the HammerLanding countdown value assigned to fp->mv.co.hammerlanding.x4.
smart_moves: Created a focused ftCo_HammerLanding.c and .h rather than leaving matched functions declared in the catch-all ft_0C31.h, improving module locality for future ftCommon hammer work.
Preserved address comments in the new header, making the moved symbols searchable and easy to correlate with the original DOL addresses.
Kept the remaining unmatched ft_0C31.s code in the build while inserting the new C object immediately before it, a typical safe transition pattern for partial file decompilation.
Used a dedicated hammerlanding motion-vars member instead of reusing unrelated union fields, documenting that fp+2344 is a float countdown/state value for this action.
Changed p_ftCommonData->x6B4 to int based on how the original assembly loaded it and converted/stored it into the HammerLanding float field.
Left an explicit todo in ftCo_800C5F88 noting that the flags declaration cannot move below fp, suggesting a suspected inline/order dependency rather than hiding the matching constraint.
decomp_lessons: When moving a matched action from a larger asm file, remove both the text symbols and any constants that are only used by the matched C code from the asm file.
Small action-state files can often be matched by isolating action entry, Anim, IASA, Phys, and Coll callbacks into a dedicated ftCommon module.
Motion variable unions should be extended with action-specific structs when an action uses fp+2340/fp+2344-style scratch fields; this makes the code more readable and supports matching.

Type refinements in shared structs such as ftCommonData can be justified by observed use in matched code, especially when an unknown field participates in integer-to-float conversion/countdown behavior.
For simple callbacks, direct wrappers can match: HammerLanding_Phys calls ft_80084F3C, HammerLanding_Coll delegates to ftCo_HammerWait_Coll, and HammerLanding_IASA is an empty function.
Declaration placement matters for decomp hygiene: once a symbol has a specific module header, remove stale prototypes from broad placeholder headers.
naming_conventions: New module follows ftCommon action naming: ftCo_HammerLanding.c and ftCo_HammerLanding.h.
Callback functions use the action callback suffix convention: ftCo_HammerLanding_Anim, ftCo_HammerLanding_IASA, ftCo_HammerLanding_Phys, ftCo_HammerLanding_Coll.
The action entry/helper function remains address-based as ftCo_800C5F88 because no semantic name was established in the PR evidence.
The include guard uses the address-style project convention GALE01_0C5F88.
The new motion-vars union member is named hammerlanding and its fields remain x0/x4 because the PR did not establish stronger semantic field names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched ftCo_800C5F88 by declaring u32 flags before retrieving Fighter* fp; the source comment says moving flags below fp breaks matching and suggests an inline-related ordering constraint.

The original assembly loaded p_ftCommonData->x6B4, performed signed integer-to-float conversion using S32_TO_F32, and stored the result at fp+2344; the C represents this as assigning an int common-data value into a float hammerlanding.x4 field.

ftCo_800C5F88 preserves call order: set hammerlanding.x4, call ftCo_800C54C4 for flags, call ftCo_800D5AEC with ftCo_MS_HammerLanding and Ft_MF_KeepFastFall plus ftCo_800C548C(fp), then call ftCo_800C4E94(fp).
ftCo_HammerLanding_Anim preserves the countdown behavior: call ftCo_800C4F64, branch to ftCo_800C4ED8 when hammerlanding.x4 <= 0, then decrement hammerlanding.x4.
Void wrapper callbacks were expressed minimally to match small 32-byte assembly functions, avoiding unnecessary temporaries in Phys and Coll.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename ftCo_800C5F88 or fields hammerlanding.x0/x4 with semantic names.
Search for additional ftCommon hammer action matches that use p_ftCommonData->x6B4 or similar countdown fields.
Check later reviews or commits for resolution of the todo about flags ordering and suspected inline behavior in ftCo_800C5F88.
Compare HammerLanding with HammerFall, HammerWait, HammerJump, and HammerKneeBend to identify shared helper patterns and possible inline candidates.
